title: QuickAdapt Universal Task
description: Normalized representation of a task/to-do/ticket from any project management platform. This is the intermediate format that all source adapters must output. It preserves structured data before transformation into the Glean Indexing API payload.

source.description: Identifies where this task came from. Critical for traceability — when something looks wrong in Glean, you need to trace it back to the originating platform and record.

platform.description: Lowercase identifier for the source platform (e.g., 'asana', 'clickup', 'basecamp'). Used to route back to the correct adapter if re-syncing is needed.

platformTaskId.description: The task's native ID in the source platform. Preserved exactly as-is — no transformation. This is your audit trail.

id.description: QuickAdapt's normalized ID. Alphanumeric only — this is a Glean constraint. Generated by combining the platform name and native ID (e.g., 'asana' + '1234567' = 'asana1234567'). This ensures uniqueness across platforms while conforming to Glean's ID restrictions.

title.description: The task title. Maps directly to Glean's document 'title' field. Every platform has this — it's the most universal field.

description.description: The task body/description. Will become part of Glean's 'body.textContent' — this is what makes the task searchable. Some platforms use rich text (HTML/markdown); adapters should strip to plain text.

url.description: Direct link to the task in the source platform. Maps to Glean's 'viewURL'. When someone finds this task in Glean search, this is where they click through to.

status.description: Current task status, lowercased and normalized. Each adapter maps platform-specific statuses to a common set: 'open', 'in_progress', 'review', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'unknown'. This becomes a Glean customProperty for filtering.

priority.description: Task priority, normalized to: 'critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low', 'none'. Becomes a Glean customProperty. Not all platforms have this — 'none' is the safe default.

assignee.description: Who is responsible for this task. Nullable because not all tasks are assigned. Email is the key field — Glean uses email for identity resolution across its connectors.

email.description: Email address. This is how Glean maps this person to an identity in its knowledge graph.

project.description: The parent project/list/space this task belongs to. Naming varies wildly across platforms ('project' in Asana, 'space' in ClickUp, 'todolist' in Basecamp). We normalize to just 'project'. Becomes a Glean customProperty.

tags.description: Labels/tags attached to the task. Concatenated into the body text for searchability in Glean, since Glean doesn't have a native tags field on documents.

dueDate.description: ISO 8601 due date. Becomes a Glean customProperty. Nullable — not all tasks have deadlines.

createdAt.description: When the task was created in the source platform. ISO 8601.

updatedAt.description: When the task was last modified in the source platform. ISO 8601. Used to determine if a re-sync is needed.

syncedAt.description: When QuickAdapt last pulled this task. This is OUR timestamp, not the source platform's. Critical for debugging sync issues — 'when did we last look at this?'
